results.title: Underage
results.creator_credit: Kim Longinotto, Lizzie Lemon and Lemon Film
results.brief_description: A look at a group of eight teenagers - all in the 15/16 age group - who live in Coventry. The film traces the pressures of growing up without real prospects of employment and it examines the teenagers’ need to prove their identity. Subjects discussed include, racism, glue-sniffing, petty theft and promiscuity.

results.title: Grain elevator
results.creator_credit: Charles Konawal and Wayne Finucan Productions
results.brief_description: Basic film about the workings of an old family style grain mill, a one to two man operation. One hundred and fifty box cars per year of grain are provided from about 100 small farmers.

results.title: Dories
results.creator_credit: Mike Mahoney, Ted Haley, Rex Tasker and Whitman Trecartin
results.brief_description: Captain Albert Crouse, narrator of the film, used dories for fishing off the coast of Lunenberg, Nova Scotia. The film sketches the history of the dory. A local craftsman gives a step-by-step demonstration of the vessel’s construction

results.title: Suzdal
results.brief_description: A tour of the unique museum-town of Suzdal. This town, a treasure of Russian culture, is the home of more than seventy architectural masterpieces of the 13th to the 19th centuries. Introduction and some credits in English. Remainder of film musical background only.

results.title: Mickey's Christmas carol
results.creator_credit: Burny Mattinson and Walt Disney Productions
results.brief_description: Mickey’s Christmas Carol features Scrooge McDuck as the miserly Ebenezer Scrooge, Goofy as a rather accident-prone ghost of Jacob Marley; Jiminy Cricket as the ghost of Christmas Past, and a host of other Disney favourites! It’s all the magic of Disney animation in an enchanting new version of the world’s best-loved Christmas fantasy. Rated: G

results.title: Boys and girls
results.creator_credit: Don McBrearty, Janice Platt, Michael Macmillan, Seaton McLean and Atlantis Productions
results.brief_description: Set in the 1940’s on a fox farm, this film explores the critical period in a young girl’s life when she first realises the existence and implications of traditional role stereotyping. At thirteen, Margaret enjoys helping her father with the outdoor labour, but she soon realises that her parents consider her help necessary only until their son is old enough to take over. When her attempts to express her feelings fail and she sees the favoured treatment of her younger brother she rebels and lets loose a rogue mare destined for slaughter. Provides a valuable study of the subtle ways in which sex role stereotyping can occur. Based on a story by Alice Munro. Suitable for secondary school level.

results.title: Marathon the ultimate challenge
results.creator_credit: Alan Jacques, Patrick Ramsey and Marathon Film Productions
results.brief_description: An award winning film about the determination, the satisfaction and the exhilaration of running a marathon. This film takes the viewer into a marathon and gives insight into what a runner experiences. This is more than a sports film - it is a film about setting a goal, working towards it and achieving it.

results.title: Mon oncle Antoine
results.creator_credit: Claude Jutra, Marc Beaudet, Gendon Films and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: There was a time when the general store was the crossroads of life, a place where a boy could learn all he needed for the way ahead - especially when his uncle was the storekeeper, and also the undertaker, and the nephew often called upon to lend a hand. This film recalls such a store in a village in the asbestos mining area of Quebec in the early 1940s. The film presents a hundred-and-one vignettes of village life - all the bitter-sweet nostalgia with which a man might remember the events that thrust him into manhood. Mon oncle Antoine is about a Quebec that makes no headlines but reflects the whole of life, the ebb and flow of hope and despair that might be in anyone’s memory. (Awards: Fifteen awards including Moscow; Chicago; Toronto) In French with English subtitles.

results.title: Tomorrow winter comes
results.creator_credit: Therese Dumesnil, Francois Seguillon, Marc Beaudet and National Film Board of Canada
results.brief_description: More and more skiers are opting for cross-country skiing rather than schussing down the crowded, and costlier, maintained slopes. This film captures the full pleasure and delight of this more relaxed but equally invigorating winter sport. A small group of skiers is observed on a day’s outing and, as they glide along through quiet Laurentian woodlands, the film notes techniques of movement. Tips are also given on proper clothing and on ski maintenance. In French with English subtitles. (Award: La Baule.)
